diagrams.title: Hierarchical Directory Structure
diagrams.description: A tree-like visualization showing how directories branch from the root directory (/), demonstrating parent-child relationships, subdirectories, and files at various levels. This helps students understand how modern file systems organize data in a hierarchical manner.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the hierarchical organization of directories in modern operating systems, showing path relationships and directory nesting

diagrams.title: Directory Entry Structure
diagrams.description: A detailed view of what information is stored in each directory entry, showing fields like filename, file extension, attributes, inode/FCB pointer, file size, timestamps, and cluster/blocks. This demystifies how the OS tracks files.
diagrams.purpose: Shows the internal structure of directory entries and what metadata the OS stores for each file

diagrams.title: Absolute vs Relative Path Resolution
diagrams.description: A comparison showing how the same file can be accessed via absolute path (from root) and relative path (from current working directory), with visual arrows showing the traversal sequence through the directory tree.
elements: Start and end points clearly marked
diagrams.purpose: Explains the difference between absolute and relative paths and how the OS resolves file locations
